title: NOC 94107 – Machine operators of other metal products | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s fabricated metal product manufacturing sector is a high-volume industry supplying everything from precision fasteners to structural components for green energy projects. Machine Operators of Other Metal Products (NOC 94107) are the versatile technicians responsible for operating the specialized machinery that mass-produces these essential goods. As Canada reshores its manufacturing to secure domestic supply chains, international workers with experience in automated assembly and high-speed metal processing are seeing a significant rise in job offers.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group941 – Processing and manufacturing machine operators

duties: Machine Operation: Operating and monitoring machines that produce a wide variety of metal products such as wire, cables, nails, and springs.
Setup & Adjustment: Loading metal coils or bars into machines and adjusting settings for speed, tension, and product dimensions.
Quality Inspection: Using digital calipers and visual inspection systems to ensure products meet high-precision engineering standards.
Minor Maintenance: Clearing jams, replacing worn tool bits, and performing routine lubrication of moving parts.
2026 Update: Operating AI-enhanced sorting systems that use computer vision to detect microscopic defects in high-volume production runs.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Experience: Some previous experience as a labourer or helper in the same company is usually preferred.
Training: Several weeks of on-the-job training are standard for new operators.
Certifications: WHMIS 2026 and site-specific machine guarding safety training.
Physical Stamina: Ability to handle repetitive tasks and stand for long periods in a manufacturing environment.
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) expects over 10,000 new job openings in this sector over the next decade. While automation increases efficiency, the complexity of modern metal products ensures that skilled human operators remain the most vital part of the production floor.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 labor market for miscellaneous metal product operators is rated as Moderate to Good in Canada’s industrial heartlands:
Ontario (GTA & Southwestern Ontario): Good prospects. This region hosts the highest density of hardware and metal product manufacturers in North America.
Quebec (Montreal & Montérégie): Stable demand. Focused on manufacturers of aerospace fasteners and specialized industrial components.
Manitoba: Good demand. Specifically in Winnipeg, where metal fabrication supports a thriving agricultural and transport machinery sector.
Alberta: Steady growth in the production of metal components for the renewable energy (wind/solar) and construction sectors.
Why Work in Canada as a Metal Product Operator?
Versatile Career Path: Skills gained here are transferable across hundreds of different manufacturing sub-sectors.
Technological Integration: Operate 2026-grade automated cold-heading machines, high-speed wire drawers, and robotic assembly units.
High Employment Stability: Fabricated metal products are required across construction, automotive, and consumer goods, ensuring year-round employment.
Path to PR: Frequently prioritized in Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) lists for semi-skilled workers.
pathways: Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): NOC 94107 is an eligible occupation for those with a valid job offer in Ontario’s thriving manufacturing corridor.
TR to PR Pathway (April 2026): This role is a prime candidate for the 2026 transition stream, which helps temporary workers in "Essential Manufacturing" obtain permanent residency.
Manitoba Skilled Worker in Manitoba Stream: A reliable pathway for workers who have completed 6 months of employment in the province.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Many metal product factories are located in regional hubs where the RNIP offers a community-driven path to PR.
